summary: Improvement cycle addressing external reviewer berendt's feedback on CC-0081. Changes: (1) apply-success branch in reconcile_fernet.go:97-103 and reconcile_credential.go:107-113 now sets Reason='FernetKeysRotated'/'CredentialKeysRotated' with message 'rotation applied; staging secret cleared' (was stale copy from steady-state path); tests updated to assert new Reason/Message (W-001). (2) docs/how-to/keystone-key-rotation.md and docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md add a ~60s key-rollover window callout flagged as pre-existing (CC-0074), not a CC-0081 regression (W-002). (3) Polish: event phrasing aligned to 'active keys' in docs (I-002); ensureStagingSecret doc comment documents 409-requeue behavior during rotation windows (I-003); integration tests extracted setupRotationEnvTest helper to eliminate ~90 lines of duplicated setup (I-004); RotationCompletedAnnotation gets //nolint:gosec annotation for symmetry with StagingSecretLabelKey (I-005). I-001 (tighten maxKeys bound from +1 to exact) intentionally deferred as optional ASK per reviewer.

tests_checklist.item: Tests updated to assert new Reason/Message on apply-success branch (reconcile_fernet_test.go:986-1000, reconcile_credential_test.go:1052-1063)
setupRotationEnvTest helper preserves per-test namespace isolation via distinct GenerateName prefixes
All three envtest rotation tests (EndToEnd, RejectsMalformedKeys, ReplacesDisjointIndices) migrated to helper without behavioral change
Existing RBAC, apply-output, and event-emission tests still pass without modification

code_quality_checklist.item: Condition Reason now matches emitted event name on apply-success branch (CredentialKeysRotated/FernetKeysRotated)
Helper extraction eliminates ~90 lines of duplicated envtest setup across three tests
Doc comment on ensureStagingSecret accurately describes the 409-requeue race and preservation invariant
//nolint:gosec annotations symmetric across both exported constants in rotation_staging.go

security_checklist.item: No new security surface introduced by condition message or doc changes
RBAC split (read-only main Secret, get+patch staging) preserved unchanged
Annotation-gated apply path unchanged — still requires RFC3339 annotation + validateRotationOutput pass

architecture_checklist.item: Apply-success Reason/Message reflect the just-applied transition, not steady state (matches new review pattern match-condition-reason-to-emitted-event-on-state-transitions.md)
Subsequent reconcile after apply returns applied=false (staging deleted), proceeds through steady-state path setting 'Available' Reason — no stuck condition state
Feature ID CC-0081 referenced in all new code comments, test comments, and doc callouts

dry_yagni_checklist.item: Shared setupRotationEnvTest helper replaces three copies of namespace/prerequisite/Keystone/drive/re-fetch setup
No speculative abstraction — helper takes only nsGenerateName, other knobs inlined in each test

fail_fast_checklist.item: Helper errors at setup (re-fetch) are reported with explicit message pointing to CC-0081

defensive_checklist.item: 409-requeue race between CronJob PATCH and controllerutil.CreateOrUpdate is documented; mutator does not touch secret.Data so CronJob payload is preserved on retry

suggested_improvements: docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md condition tables at lines 695 and 868 only list the steady-state Reason 'FernetKeysAvailable'/'CredentialKeysAvailable'. Consider adding a row for the transient 'FernetKeysRotated'/'CredentialKeysRotated' Reason used on the apply-success short-circuit path so the reference table matches what operators will actually see via kubectl describe immediately after a rotation apply. This was not flagged by the external reviewer but is the natural follow-through on the W-001 fix.

I-001 (tighten maxKeys bound from normalizedMaxActiveKeys+1 to exact normalizedMaxActiveKeys) is intentionally deferred per the external reviewer's optional/ASK classification. Track as a follow-up after confirming with the Keystone team that no supported version leaves a transient extra key mid-rotation.
next_steps: Merge this improvement cycle — external reviewer berendt's W-001 and W-002 are fully addressed with matching test updates, and polish items I-002/I-003/I-004/I-005 are handled.
Optional follow-up: update the reference docs condition tables to document the transient 'Rotated' Reason variant.
